{"step_by_step_thinking": "Biomineralization involves the incorporation of inorganic metals into biological structures. The pinna is an external ear structure and not a biological structure formed through biomineralization. Hooves of cattle are made primarily of keratin and do not involve significant biomineralization. Honeycomb is a structure formed by bees and not a biological structure involving biomineralization. Mollusc shells, however, are well-known examples of biomineralization, as they are primarily composed of calcium carbonate (calcite or aragonite), which is a result of biomineralization processes.", "answer_choice": "D"}
